blocks.text: Psalm 116
Not to us, O Lord, not to us, but to your name give glory, for the sake of your steadfast love and your faithfulness.

Ps 115: Show forth your glory to the nations. A community petition that the in the heavens (v. 3), too powerful to be contained in earthly statues (vv. 4–8), might display his glory on earth and enable Israel to live in trust (vv. 9–18).
1 : , better, “Not for our sake, O , not for our sake, but for the sake of your name display your glory.” The Heb phrase, lit., “to give glory,” actually means “display glory” as in Ezek 39.21.
Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?”
2 : Enemies taunt that Israel’s God is powerless.
Our God is in the heavens; he does whatever he pleases.
3 : , a bold response to the taunt (v. 2). Ridicule of divine images is common in exilic texts and later (e.g., Jer 10.1–16; Isa 40.18–20; 41.6–7; Bel; Let Jer).
Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
They have mouths, but do not speak; eyes, but do not see.
They have ears, but do not hear; noses, but do not smell.
They have hands, but do not feel; feet, but do not walk; they make no sound in their throats.
Those who make them are like them; so are all who trust in them.
3–8 : There are two contrasts: (1) between our God (singular) in the heavens and their gods (images, plural) on earth; (2) between God who acts and the gods who cannot.
O Israel, trust in the Lord! He is their help and their shield.
9 : Those who trust in images will be frustrated (v. 8) while those who will find a and (vv. 9–11).
O house of Aaron, trust in the Lord! He is their help and their shield.
10 : , the priests, who claimed Aaron as their ancestor.
You who fear the Lord, trust in the Lord! He is their help and their shield.
The Lord has been mindful of us; he will bless us; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron;
he will bless those who fear the Lord, both small and great.
12–13 : The psalmist turns to God, asking for a blessing on the same three groups who were just exhorted (vv. 10–11) to trust in the .
May the Lord give you increase, both you and your children.
May you be blessed by the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
The heavens are the Lord’s heavens, but the earth he has given to human beings.
The dead do not praise the Lord, nor do any that go down into silence.
17 : According to the biblical worldview, all who died went down to Sheol, the underworld, where all was quiet; the dead could thus be called those . See also Ps 6.5n.
But we will bless the Lord from this time on and forevermore. Praise the Lord!
